name: Maktoob
description: The world's first free Arabic/English web-based email service, founded in Amman, Jordan. Grew into a major Arabic media portal with 16.5 million unique users. Acquired by Yahoo in 2009 for approximately $164 million — the first major Arab internet acquisition.

name: Aramex
description: Global logistics and express delivery company co-founded in Amman, Jordan by Fadi Ghandour. First Arab company listed on NASDAQ (1997). Pioneer in e-commerce logistics in the Middle East. Now operates in 70+ countries with 18,000+ employees.

name: Tamatem Games
description: Leading Arabic mobile game publisher and developer based in Amman, Jordan. Founded by Hussam Hammo, the company publishes localized mobile games for the Arab world. Over 40 published games with 50+ million downloads. Raised $22.5M total funding.

name: Mawdoo3
description: The world's largest Arabic content website, founded in Amman. A comprehensive online encyclopedia offering articles on health, education, cooking, and more. Serves over 50 million unique visitors monthly. Raised $13.5M in Series B funding. Parent company also spun off Qalam AI.

name: Kharabeesh
description: Jordanian digital media company and platform for digital creativity, known for animated cartoons, humor, and satire. Produces and curates original Arabic content including comedy, animation, tech, and more for youth audiences across the Arab world.

name: Jamalon
description: The Arab world's largest online bookstore, founded in Amman by Ala' Alsallal. Offers over 12 million titles including 150,000+ in Arabic. Has distribution centers in Amman, Beirut, Dubai, and London. Pioneer in print-on-demand for the MENA region.

name: Maysalward
description: The first mobile game development company in the Middle East, founded in Amman by Nour Khrais. Created the very first Arabic mobile game in 2004. Pioneer in mobile gaming localization and culturalization for the MENA market. The company's founder was honored by King Abdullah II.

name: ArabiaWeather
description: The leading private weather company serving the Arab world, founded in Amman by Mohammed Al-Shaker. Delivers weather forecasts to 70 million Arab users daily through consumer platforms, media products, and enterprise solutions across sectors like aviation, oil & gas, agriculture, and insurance.

name: Altibbi
description: MENA's leading telehealth and medical information platform, founded by Jalil Allabadi. Provides telehealth consultations, medical information, and chronic care management to millions across the Arab world. Raised $6.5M+ in funding.

name: Abjjad
description: The Arab world's leading digital reading and publishing platform, often described as the Arab world's answer to Kindle. Features over 20,000 Arabic eBooks connecting readers, authors, and publishers. Has over 50 Arab publishers on the platform.

name: Jeeran
description: Jordanian local review and recommendations platform helping Arab users discover, review, and interact with places in their cities. Operates in 17+ Arabic cities across Jordan, KSA, UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Egypt and more.

name: MadfooatCom
description: Jordan's first e-payments company, established with investment from Oasis500. Provides bill payments, online payments, and e-payment solutions to disrupt cash payments in emerging markets. A major player in Jordan's digital payment landscape.

name: Abwaab
description: Jordanian online learning platform providing interactive and personalized educational experiences for secondary-school students. Allows students to learn at their own pace with expert tutors. Acquired Pakistan's Edmatrix platform for regional expansion.

name: Edraak
description: The first pan-Arab non-profit MOOC (Massive Open Online Course) platform, initiated by the Queen Rania Foundation. Provides free online courses in Arabic across diverse fields. Developed K-12 digital educational resources with $3M Google.org grant support.

name: Little Thinking Minds
description: Award-winning Jordanian EdTech company dedicated to closing the Arabic literacy gap for K-12 students. Serves 450,000+ students in 10+ Arab countries through educational videos, songs, digital books, and apps. Founded by Lamia Tabbaa and Rama Kayyali. Acquired by Seesaw (US).

name: Kutubee
description: Interactive reading management platform for elementary and middle school students. Provides 1,600+ stories in Arabic, English, and French for children aged 3-14. Features read-aloud recordings and comprehension tools. Accredited in 22+ countries.

name: Wajeez
description: Jordanian app providing audio and text summaries of non-fiction books in Arabic. Offers 3,500+ audio and text summaries. Reached 800,000 users in its first seven months. Raised $3M in pre-Series A funding led by STV.

name: Capifly
description: Jordan-based revenue-based financing platform offering the first online Sharia-compliant non-dilutive capital for startups in the MENA region. Founded by Ahmed Jaradat and Dunya Bashiti. Provides up to $1.5M based on Annual Recurring Revenue.

name: Dinarak
description: App-based mobile wallet for payments in Jordan, enabling users to withdraw cash, transfer money, pay bills, and pay at online and offline retailers. Operates on the JoMoPay national mobile payments switch for interoperability.

name: InvoiceQ
description: Jordan-based e-invoicing SaaS platform for businesses. Integrates with ERP systems to automate invoicing, ensure compliance with e-invoicing regulations (ZATCA Saudi Arabia, ISTD Jordan), and streamline payments. Co-founded by Fadi Aljabali, Amer Ayyad, and Muhannad Tobal.

name: A2A
description: Jordanian company providing banking software suites to banks and financial institutions. Solutions include omnichannel platform, white-label wallet, mobile banking, wearable banking, and chatbot banking.

name: Solfeh
description: Jordan-based micro-lending fintech startup providing same-day emergency cash advances to salaried employees. Fully automated platform delivering $400-$1,400 in under 30 minutes. Raised $600K total from Edgo VC, Oasis500, and others.

name: BeeezCrowd
description: Jordan's equity-based crowdfunding platform connecting investors with Micro SMEs that want to raise funds through digital shares. Enables businesses to get funding from a large number of investors.

name: WebTeb
description: Online mobile and web-based health information platform providing evidence-based medical information in Arabic. Includes a physician directory covering the Arabic-speaking market. Founded in Amman by Mahmoud Kaiyal.

name: Electronic Health Solutions (EHS)
description: Private technology-driven non-profit company advancing Jordan's healthcare sector. Operator of the national 'Hakeem' digital health transformation program, integrating health IT across public hospitals. Also runs the Health Data Analytics platform using AI and big data.

name: NatHealth
description: Jordan's first electronic Third Party Administrator (TPA) in the Middle East and the largest in Jordan. Processes over 12 million claims annually, serves 400,000+ beneficiaries. Uses AI for fraud detection and has transitioned to fully paperless operations.

name: OpenSooq
description: The largest mobile-first classifieds marketplace in MENA with over 800 million user visits annually. Enables buying and selling of cars, real estate, electronics, fashion, and more. Based in Amman, Jordan.

name: Dumyah
description: Jordan's leading e-commerce platform for women, founded in 2016. Offers maternity products, baby gear, beauty essentials, home and pet supplies, and toys. Built as a one-stop-shop to save women time and effort through doorstep delivery.

name: Repzo
description: Mobile CRM and sales force automation platform for FMCG and pharmaceutical companies. Turns smartphones into field force management tools. Founded by Hassan Atmeh, Moutaz Atmeh, and Ayman Atmeh. Raised $1.4M bridge round.

name: Gamiphy
description: User engagement and retention platform equipping brands with branded mini games, playable ads, and digital loyalty programs. Integrates into existing websites and apps. Fully DIY, customizable gamification tools for improving customer journeys.

name: Ustazi
description: Jordan-based mobile app connecting students with private tutors. Students can find, book, and rate private tutors across various subjects and levels.

name: GasNas
description: Jordanian mobile app for on-demand gas cylinder delivery to homes. Provides quick and efficient domestic gas delivery services.

name: Tikram
description: Jordanian super app offering multiple services including ride-hailing, gas supply, water supply, goods transport, and flight ticket booking.

name: Petra Ride
description: App-based ride-hailing platform operating across multiple Jordanian governorates including Amman, Karak, Irbid, Salt, Madaba, Zarqa, and Mafraq. Offers real-time tracking and insured rides.

name: NakalBoard
description: Jordanian logistics truck and equipment rental platform connecting shippers, carriers, and equipment providers. Uses AI-powered solutions to streamline freight transport and equipment rental.

name: Vardot
description: One of the world's top Drupal development shops, headquartered in Amman. Specializes in complex content-heavy websites for NGOs, universities, and media organizations. 350+ projects in 30+ countries. Clients include UNICEF, UN, and Georgetown University. Founded by Mohammed Razem.

name: Aspire
description: Premier quality assurance and software testing company in Jordan, founded in 2002. CEO Emad Tabaza. 500+ employees. Serves major US media and publishing companies including Cisco, Expedia, and HP. Also provides custom software development, web and mobile apps, and cloud services.

name: Sprintive
description: Amman-based web and mobile development company specializing in web development, UX/UI design, SEO, and Drupal consulting. Products include Sprintive Guard, SprintHire, and Musaed.

name: Jordan Data Systems (JDS)
description: One of Jordan's oldest and most established IT companies, providing end-to-end IT solutions including software, data centers, networking, and communication systems. Part of the Midis Group, the largest IT group in MENA/Eastern Europe. 600+ satisfied customers.

name: Inspire for Solutions Development
description: Distinguished IT consulting firm providing enterprise IT solutions across Jordan, Egypt, KSA, Qatar, Oman, and the UAE. Team of 450+ IT professionals specializing in IBM, Atlassian, security, BPM, and enterprise platforms.

name: Cryptika
description: Amman-based cybersecurity company founded in 2016, offering penetration testing, vulnerability assessments, security architecture reviews, threat hunting, digital forensics, IT audits, and ISO 27001 implementation. Offices in Jordan, UAE, and KSA.

name: Hayyan Horizons
description: Specialized cybersecurity system integrator and consulting firm based in Amman, Jordan. Established in 2015, serves clients across the Middle East, Africa, and Europe. Offers penetration testing, red teaming, incident response, and best-in-class information security services.

name: Threatsys
description: Leading cybersecurity consulting partner based in Amman, Jordan. Specializes in securing IT infrastructure for leading enterprises globally. Services include cybersecurity consulting, testing, compliance, forensics, managed security, and 360-degree cybersecurity-as-a-service.

name: Qalam AI
description: AI-powered Arabic writing assistant and NLP platform spun off from Mawdoo3. First Arabic writing digital assistant offering grammar checks, spelling corrections, alternative suggestions, and punctuation reviews for Arabic text. Founded by Rami Al-Qawasmi.

name: Oasis500
description: Jordan's first entrepreneurial catalyst and early-stage seed investor, founded in 2010. Has invested in 193+ startups that collectively raised $135M+ in follow-on funding. Active portfolio valued at $243M. Portfolio companies operate in 22+ countries and have generated 5,000 jobs.

name: iPARK
description: Jordan's leading innovation catalyst creating opportunities for entrepreneurs, industries, and government to amplify ideas and build successful technology businesses. Supports economic and social growth of Jordan through incubation and innovation programs.

name: ZINC (Zain Innovation Campus)
description: Jordan's first startup enabler, launched by Zain Jordan in 2014 at King Hussein Business Park. Offers co-working space and incubation services. Officially launched by King Abdullah II. Now operates across 9 branches nationwide.

name: TTi Ventures
description: Non-profit Jordanian organization dedicated to strengthening the entrepreneurship ecosystem. Founded in 2010, operates across micro (entrepreneurs), meso (support organizations), and macro (policy) levels.

name: Silicon Badia
description: Global venture capital firm based in Amman, Jordan, investing in early-stage technology companies. Focuses on AI/ML, fintech, proptech, developer tools, and enterprise software. Made 106 investments with 50 exits. Cisco-backed. Partner in the $10M BeyondCapital fund with Endeavor Jordan and USAID.

name: Wamda Capital
description: MENA's influential entrepreneurship platform and venture capital fund, founded by Fadi Ghandour (Aramex founder) in 2010. Invested in 100+ companies including Maktoob, Souq, Careem, and Tamatem. Provides research, community development, and ecosystem support. $70M Fund 1.

name: Orange Jordan
description: Jordan's leading telecom operator, formerly known as Jordan Telecom. Part of Orange Group operating in 26 countries. Launched Jordan's 5G network with widest coverage in the Kingdom. Also operates BIG accelerator for startups.

name: Zain Jordan
description: Leading telecom operator in Jordan since 1995, part of the regional Zain Group. Leads the Jordanian market with over 46% subscriber share. Provides mobile, internet, and digital services. Operates ZINC innovation campus.

name: Umniah
description: Third telecom operator in Jordan, operating since 2005 as a Batelco subsidiary. Holds 26.5% subscriber share. Offers mobile, fiber-optic internet, and 5G. Operates 'The Tank' startup incubator and Plug and Play acceleration program.

name: Roya TV
description: One of Jordan's leading private satellite TV channels owned by the Al-Sayegh Family. Broadcasts from Jordan Media City studios. A major presence in Jordanian digital media and television broadcasting.

name: Phytobase
description: Jordan-based tech-driven company providing smart, integrated solutions for sustainable agriculture. Core offering is a smart irrigation system using environmental sensors (soil moisture, temperature, light) and IoT technologies (LoRa) with AI-powered digital platform.

name: Khudra Connect
description: Jordanian digital agri-platform empowering farmers through smart tools to improve crop productivity and connect directly with markets. Uses data analytics and demand forecasting to reduce post-harvest losses and increase farmer profitability.

name: Bayanat
description: Jordanian fintech company specializing in digital transformation for banks and financial institutions. Provides payment systems and cloud migration solutions.

name: JOPACC
description: Jordan Payments and Clearing Company — the national payment infrastructure operator in Jordan. Oversees interbank payments, national payment switch, and digital payment ecosystem development.

name: Ahli Fintech
description: Jordan's first fintech company fully owned by a licensed bank (Ahli Bank), established in 2017. Vision to promote the fintech ecosystem and establish Jordan as a regional fintech hub. Invested in InvoiceQ among others.

name: SAMA Telecom
description: Jordanian provider of GPS-based fleet and asset tracking services using proprietary GPS devices. Offers delivery tracking, personal tracking, pet tracking, ATM monitoring, taxi tracking, vehicle camera systems, and fleet management.

name: FittiCoin
description: Jordanian wellness app tracking fitness activity and converting it into virtual coins redeemable for rewards, discounts, or fitness products. Gamified fitness platform to encourage healthier lifestyles.

name: Coders
description: Leading tech company based in Amman specializing in Drupal services, mobile apps, Flutter, e-commerce, and startup solutions. Known for high-quality full-stack development across web and mobile platforms.

name: Startappz
description: Custom software development company in Jordan renowned for expertise in mobile application development, backend development, and web development services. Over 90% of reviewers highlight high-quality deliverables.

name: Geel Tech
description: Leading IT services provider in Jordan specializing in web apps and multi-platform mobile app development since 2010.

name: Synkers
description: Jordan-based software company developing MVPs, proofs of concept, and fast-iteration prototypes to test business assumptions before larger capital investment.

name: Sprintive
description: Amman-based web and mobile development firm founded in 2016. Specializes in high-quality website design, Drupal consulting, SEO-driven content creation, and UX/UI design. Products include Sprintive Guard and Musaed.

name: Endeavor Jordan
description: Jordan chapter of Endeavor, the global high-impact entrepreneur network. Provides mentorship, capital connections, and strategic support to high-potential Jordanian entrepreneurs. Partner in the $10M BeyondCapital fund with Silicon Badia and USAID.

name: Accelerate Jordan
description: Jordanian startup acceleration program supporting entrepreneurs and the local entrepreneurial ecosystem, offering programs for startups across multiple sectors.

name: Roya Media Group
description: One of Jordan's major private media groups. Operates satellite and digital television channels, with production studios at Jordan Media City. Active in digital content and online media.

name: Factosecure
description: Comprehensive cybersecurity company in Jordan for 2026, blending advanced threat intelligence, real-time monitoring, and strategic advisory to build resilient security ecosystems for businesses.

name: Codexal
description: Forward-thinking software engineering firm positioned as an innovation engine for clients in Jordan. Delivers AI software development including machine learning, NLP, and computer vision solutions for global clients.

name: Jordan Data Network
description: IT solutions provider operating since 1995, working with startups and established businesses to meet all IT requirements including networking, infrastructure, and data services.

name: Quality Business Solutions (QBS)
description: Leading technology services provider in Jordan offering business solutions and consultation with focus in governmental, telecommunication, financial, and industrial sectors.

name: HASSAD Agritech Accelerator
description: Jordan's first AgriTech accelerator, supporting startups addressing agricultural challenges including water scarcity, food security, and crop productivity in Jordan and the region.

name: Bayt.com
description: The Middle East's leading job portal, founded in Dubai with Jordan as a key operational market. Connects job seekers and employers across the MENA region.

name: DoorBox
description: Jordanian delivery platform providing last-mile delivery solutions for businesses and consumers.

name: Rased
description: Jordanian media monitoring and analytics platform tracking online and traditional media mentions for brands and organizations across the Arab world.

name: Ubuy Jordan
description: International online shopping platform for premium and luxury brands operating in Jordan. Provides imported goods at competitive prices with international shipping.

name: Spendwisor
description: Jordan-based personal finance management app helping users track and manage their spending and budgets.

name: SIMLAB Soft
description: Jordanian deep tech company specializing in 3D simulation and visualization software solutions for architecture, engineering, and related industries.

name: MARS Robotics
description: Jordanian deep tech startup developing robotics solutions, based in Amman.

name: Ferasah Engineering
description: Jordanian deep tech company providing advanced engineering and technology solutions.

name: Keylife
description: Jordanian deep tech startup in the health technology space, developing innovative solutions based in Amman.

name: Almaany
description: One of the leading Arabic online dictionary and translation platforms, providing comprehensive Arabic-English and multilingual dictionaries and language tools widely used across the Arab world.

name: DeepScale App
description: Jordanian mobile application using selfies and AI/machine learning to estimate body fat percentage and body composition metrics.

name: Shams Jo
description: Jordanian solar energy solutions provider offering photovoltaic installations, clean energy consulting, and solar system management for residential, commercial, and industrial clients.

name: I Read Arabic
description: Arabic language learning platform providing online reading resources including videos, online lessons, and worksheets to help learners improve Arabic reading skills.

name: Math Seven
description: Comprehensive educational technology platform enhancing mathematics education through innovative digital solutions and interactive content for students.

name: STEAM Hub Jordan
description: Educational force in Jordan's tech scene serving as a vital link between schools and educators. Focuses on Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Mathematics (STEAM) curricula.

name: GreenTech Jo
description: Jordanian agritech company specializing in hydroponics and controlled-environment agriculture solutions.

name: AgroLink Jordan
description: Digital marketplace and platform for farmers in Jordan, connecting agricultural producers with buyers and suppliers.

name: Atomkit
description: Jordanian technology company providing software development tools and solutions based in Amman.

name: TechVista
description: Jordanian company focused on developing educational technology solutions and e-learning applications. Specializes in video streaming integration and interactive content creation for learning platforms.

name: iPlant Technologies
description: Jordanian agritech startup developing smart technology solutions for plant care and agriculture.

name: Jordan Crown
description: Jordan-based web design agency and web development solutions company serving local and regional clients.

name: BIG by Orange Jordan
description: Startup acceleration program launched by Orange Jordan in 2015 at King Hussein Business Park. Offers a 120-day program with mentorship, business development training, and co-working space. No equity taken from participants.

name: Umniah The Tank
description: Business incubator operated by Umniah telecom, part of Jordan's startup ecosystem. Provides workspace and support for early-stage startups.

name: WFP Jordan Innovation Hub
description: World Food Programme innovation hub in Jordan supporting local innovations and entrepreneurs with tech solutions tackling food security issues. Leverages WFP's operational network.

name: Neiontech
description: Jordanian IT and software company based in Amman providing technology solutions and development services.

name: Tkxel Jordan
description: Software development and IT outsourcing company with a presence in Jordan, delivering custom software, mobile apps, and digital transformation solutions.

name: StartupsJo
description: Jordan's Startups Council — a platform and directory representing and supporting the Jordanian startup community. Provides visibility and networking for Jordan's tech startups.

name: Jordanict.com
description: Who's Who in Jordan's Information and Communication Technology sector — comprehensive directory of ICT companies, products, and services in Jordan.

name: Smartech Jo
description: One-place digital space provider offering technology and digital services solutions in Jordan.

name: 2Easy IT
description: Jordanian IT company specializing in e-commerce platform solutions and digital business enablement for Jordanian businesses.

name: Uptixa
description: Jordanian AI and machine learning development company providing tailored AI solutions for businesses in Jordan.

name: Arrow Engineering Solutions
description: Jordanian engineering solutions and training company providing technical education and engineering services.

name: Experia
description: Jordanian health technology and medical solutions company focused on improving healthcare delivery.

name: Kawar Group (Technology Division)
description: Jordan's leading business conglomerate with a significant healthcare and technology division. Provides medical technology and IT solutions alongside healthcare services.

name: Al Tawseel Logistics
description: Jordanian logistics and delivery tech company providing fast delivery services and warehousing solutions for businesses.

name: Jordanian Eagle for Security (JOEAGLE)
description: Comprehensive security solutions provider in Jordan offering cybersecurity and physical security solutions, delivering security as a managed service.

name: Sowtech
description: Jordanian audio and digital media technology company providing sound and multimedia services.

name: Jordan Startup Expo
description: B2B exhibition and conference for Jordan's innovative and emerging startups, providing a platform for startups to showcase to investors and partners.

name: Spendee Jordan
description: Personal finance and budgeting app available in Jordan, helping users track their expenses and manage financial goals.

name: Hsoub (Hosub)
description: Arab world's leading digital economy platform. While HQ is in Jordan, it operates platforms including Mostaql (freelance marketplace) and Hsoub Academy (online tech education), serving millions of Arabic-speaking professionals.

name: Mostaql
description: The Arab world's largest freelance marketplace, part of Hsoub. Connects Arab freelancers with employers across technology, design, writing, and business services.

name: Hsoub Academy
description: Online technical education platform in Arabic, part of the Hsoub group. Offers programming courses, web development, and technology education for Arabic speakers.

name: Goodreads Arabia
description: Arabic book discovery and social reading community — Abjjad serves as the Jordanian-created Arabic equivalent, while Goodreads operates in the region.

name: Waseeltech
description: Jordanian web and software development company providing digital solutions for businesses.

name: Exeeda
description: Jordanian digital marketing and software company providing web development, SEO, and digital marketing services.

name: Idealz
description: Social commerce platform operating in Jordan and MENA that combines online shopping with charity and prize draws.

name: Arab Open University Jordan
description: Leading distance and e-learning institution in Jordan offering technology-enhanced education across multiple disciplines.

name: Edraak K-12
description: Jordan-developed K-12 digital educational resources platform offering free open educational resources in Arabic aligned with national curricula across MENA. Built with $3M Google.org grant.

name: Luminus Education
description: Jordanian technology education and vocational training institution. Featured in WEF's top 100 Arabian startups. Focuses on preparing graduates for Jordan's tech industry.

name: Tactful AI
description: Jordanian AI company building conversational AI and customer experience automation platforms for enterprises.

name: Petra Biosciences
description: Jordanian biotech and life sciences startup developing innovative biological solutions.

name: Cura Health
description: Jordanian digital health platform providing online medical consultations and health management services.

name: HypoFarm
description: Jordanian agritech startup addressing agricultural challenges through technology innovation. Supported through EBRD's green technology track.

name: Zawya Jordan
description: Regional financial news and data platform covering Jordan's business, economic, and technology sector developments.

name: JoMoPay
description: Jordan's national mobile payment switch, enabling interoperability between licensed mobile payment service providers, mobile wallets, bank accounts, and prepaid cards.

name: Flick Digital
description: Jordanian digital marketing and creative agency providing digital strategy, social media management, and content production.

name: Proptech.jo
description: Jordanian real estate technology platform connecting property buyers, sellers, and renters through a digital marketplace.

name: DealFloor
description: Jordanian deals and discounts e-commerce platform connecting consumers with offers from local businesses.

name: Hakeem Platform (EHS)
description: Jordan's national digital health transformation program operated by Electronic Health Solutions (EHS). Integrates advanced health IT technologies to digitize all healthcare services across Jordan's public hospitals and clinics.

name: Pixel Point
description: Jordanian software and mobile app development company providing digital product development services.

name: Evolent Technologies
description: Jordan-based technology company delivering software development, IT services, and digital transformation solutions.

name: Ruwwad Digital
description: Jordanian digital agency providing web design, branding, digital marketing, and software development for businesses.

name: AppToDate
description: Jordanian mobile app development company creating consumer and enterprise applications for iOS and Android.

name: Tag Systems
description: Jordanian enterprise software and IT systems company providing ERP and technology solutions to businesses.

name: Mena Apps
description: Jordanian mobile application development company creating apps for the MENA market.

name: Eyouth
description: Jordanian social enterprise and EdTech platform connecting youth with employment and training opportunities through digital tools.

name: Bookwitty
description: Digital publishing and book discovery platform with presence in Jordan and MENA. Provides content and community for book lovers.

name: APEX Solutions
description: Jordanian enterprise software solutions company providing custom software and business technology services.

name: Progrexion (Jordan Office)
description: International technology company with a significant development center in Amman, Jordan, contributing to global software and BPO services.

name: Mena Trench
description: Digital marketing agency based in Amman, Jordan providing performance marketing, social media, and digital strategy.

name: Remal Mobile
description: Jordanian mobile technology company focused on creating innovative mobile applications and digital solutions.

name: Jordan Telecom Group (Orange)
description: Jordan's historic national telecom group, now operating as Orange Jordan. Provides fixed-line, broadband, mobile, and digital services. Part of Orange Group.

name: Foras Platform
description: Jordanian online employment and jobs platform connecting job seekers with employers across Jordan.

name: Expat Woman Jordan
description: Online community and digital platform serving expatriate women in Jordan and the MENA region.

name: Sharki
description: Jordanian services marketplace connecting consumers with local service providers across various categories.

name: Nuqtat
description: Jordanian loyalty and rewards platform connecting consumers with brands through a digital points and rewards ecosystem.

name: Makani
description: Jordanian digital mapping and address system platform improving location data and navigation in Jordan.

name: ProgressSoft
description: Leading Jordanian software company providing real-time payment and financial technology solutions to banks and financial institutions globally. Serves clients in 35+ countries.

name: Excelerate Jordan
description: Jordanian technology accelerator and training program for startups and entrepreneurs in the tech sector.

name: Meddy
description: Healthcare platform operating in Jordan and Gulf countries, helping patients find and book doctor appointments online with verified reviews.

name: Tebcan
description: Jordanian healthcare technology startup providing digital health solutions and medical information services.

name: Alpha Hub
description: Jordan-based cybersecurity company helping businesses strengthen their defenses with advanced threat intelligence solutions.

name: Greener
description: Jordanian cleantech company focused on sustainability and environmental technology solutions.

name: Pio Solutions
description: Jordanian ERP and business software solutions company providing enterprise resource planning and business management systems.

name: Souq.com (Amazon.com/sa)
description: The Arab world's largest e-commerce marketplace. Co-founded in Dubai in 2005 (with Jordanian co-founders), Souq.com grew to become the dominant online marketplace in MENA before being acquired by Amazon for $580M in 2017 and rebranded as Amazon.ae/Amazon.sa.

name: Kenz (MakanE)
description: Jordanian retail technology and e-commerce platform offering innovative shopping experiences.

name: ProgressSoft Payment Hub
description: ProgressSoft's flagship payment hub product — a real-time payment processing platform deployed in central banks and financial institutions across 35+ countries worldwide. Jordan-developed global payment technology.

name: Agrium Middle East (AgriTech)
description: Jordanian agricultural technology and solutions company providing precision agriculture services.

name: Bright by Text (Jordan Partnership)
description: Text-message-based educational tips platform for parents, with Jordanian partnerships supporting early childhood education through mobile technology.

name: Sarwa Tech
description: MENA digital wealth management platform with operations in Jordan and the UAE, offering robo-advisory investment services.

name: Zid
description: E-commerce enablement platform with strong Jordan market presence allowing merchants to build and manage online stores. Competes with Shopify in the Arab market.

name: Salla (Jordan Market)
description: Leading Arab e-commerce platform enabling Jordanian and regional merchants to create and manage online stores. Strong presence in Jordan.

name: eSolutions Jordan
description: Jordanian government and enterprise IT solutions provider specializing in digital transformation projects.

name: Umniah Business Solutions
description: Business solutions arm of Umniah telecom providing IoT, cloud connectivity, and enterprise digital services to Jordanian businesses.

name: GoPlus
description: Jordanian healthcare technology startup providing digital health and wellness solutions.

name: Arab Digital Economy
description: Jordan-based digital economy initiative and platform supporting the growth of e-commerce and digital business in the Arab world.

name: Tahaluf
description: Jordan-based tech events and media company organizing technology conferences and B2B events in the region.
